channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police find no new evidence in renewed search for missing SA boy Gus Lamont; family member remains a suspect
item.description: Eight months after four-year-old Gus Lamont disappeared from Oak Park Station near Yunta in South Australia’s far north, investigators have confirmed that a fresh round of searches has failed to uncov...

item.title: Melbourne Storm vs Sydney Roosters Round 13 NRL clash at AAMI Park – match highlights
item.description: The article covers the Round 13 fixture of the 2026 NRL Premiership between the Melbourne Storm and the Sydney Roosters, played at AAMI Park in Melbourne. The match is presented as a key encounter in ...

item.title: Sarangani drug suspect arrested in entrapment operation aided by relatives
item.description: Authorities in Sarangani province successfully arrested a 43-year-old suspected shabu dealer in a carefully planned entrapment operation that notably involved assistance from his own relatives. The op...

item.title: Opinion argues CRA applies inconsistent scrutiny to religious charities amid allegations involving Muslim Association of Canada
item.description: This opinion article by Jesse Kline discusses allegations and concerns regarding how the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) oversees religious charities in Canada. The author argues that there is an inconsis...

item.title: Health experts caution that consumption of wild animals may be contributing to Ebola outbreaks in Congo
item.description: Health experts are warning that the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo may be linked to the consumption and handling of wild animals, often referred to as bushmeat. The Associa...

item.title: UNESCO and Japan provide ICT equipment to 15 Nigerian Colleges of Education to boost teacher training in Nigeria
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don conclude arrangement to receive Information and Communication Technology (ICT) equipment and learning materials for 15 Federal and State Colleges of Education acr...

item.title: Internal emails and court testimony highlight overcrowding and safety concerns at Manhattan ICE detention facility
item.description: Newly released internal communications and court testimony have shed light on serious concerns over conditions inside a makeshift Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detention facility located o...

item.title: Wests Tigers face Canterbury Bulldogs in Round 13 NRL clash at CommBank Stadium
item.description: In Round 13 of the 2026 NRL Premiership, the Wests Tigers went head-to-head with the Canterbury Bulldogs at Sydney's CommBank Stadium. The match was an intense display of skill, strategy, and athletic...

item.title: Madras High Court permits undertrial prisoners to opt for private hospital treatment under custody conditions
item.description: The Madras High Court has ruled that undertrial prisoners have the right to receive medical treatment at private hospitals of their choice, provided no prejudice is caused to the prosecution, security...

item.title: uShaka Marine World receives R22 million municipal bailout amid financial strain
item.description: uShaka Marine World in Durban has been granted a R22 million financial bailout by the eThekwini Municipality in an effort to stabilise its ongoing financial difficulties. The decision was approved dur...

item.title: Editorial calls on Mark Carney to take stronger action against rising antisemitism in Canada
item.description: This National Post View opinion piece argues that Jewish Canadians require more than symbolic statements in response to a sharp rise in antisemitism across the country. It highlights data indicating a...

item.title: Faiba introduces Raha Jipe mobile bundles to target broader customers amid rising telecom competition
item.description: Kenyan telecommunications company Faiba has rolled out a major overhaul of its mobile and internet offerings under a new strategy branded ‘Raha Jipe’, as competition in the country’s data and voice ma...

item.title: Woman denied bail after alleged attempt to print child exploitation material at Belconnen store
item.description: A 36-year-old woman from New South Wales has been denied bail following allegations that she attempted to print child exploitation material at a photo printing business in Belconnen, Canberra. The mat...

item.title: Liverpool announce departure of head coach Arne Slot after successful spell
item.description: Liverpool Football Club don officially confirm say their head coach, Arne Slot, don leave the club with immediate effect. The announcement come on Saturday, with the club also stating say dem don alre...

item.title: EAC outlines $111 million budget to support regional integration and operations
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) has presented a proposal for an annual budget of USD 111 million aimed at strengthening regional integration and supporting its operational activities across member st...

item.title: Paul McCartney shares his experience playing on The Rolling Stones’ latest album
item.description: Paul McCartney has reflected on the thrill of collaborating with The Rolling Stones in a recent interview with NME. The Beatles legend, who is also preparing to release his 19th solo album, ‘The Boys ...

item.title: Arsenal face PSG in pivotal Champions League clash as Arteta aims to reassert European promise
item.description: Arsenal, under the direction of Mikel Arteta, confront a defining night as they host Paris Saint-Germain in a high-stakes Champions League tie. The match stands as a test of the club’s long-standing a...

item.title: EACC Arrests Kenya Power Technician and Accomplice for Soliciting Ksh30,000 Bribe to Reconnect Electricity
item.description: In Nairobi, the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) has arrested one Kenya Power employee and his accomplice after they allegedly demanded and received a bribe of Ksh30,000 to restore electri...

item.title: Highlights and Contenders of the Inaugural MGI All Stars Pageant in Bangkok
item.description: The Miss Grand International All Stars held its inaugural Grand Final on May 30, 2026, at the MGI Hall in Bangkok, Thailand. Fifty-six candidates from 38 countries competed, bringing together newcomer...

item.title: Ousted BP chair faces governance dispute following internal clashes over deal communications
item.description: BP’s recently removed chair, Albert Manifold, is reported to have been involved in internal disagreements with fellow board member Simon Henry regarding the handling of sensitive discussions about a p...
